questions.question: In polar (r, θ), r is the distance from the origin.
questions.explanation: Yes, r (radius) is always distance from center. θ is the angle.

questions.question: Which system is best for circular motion?
questions.explanation: Polar is perfect for circles. Distance stays constant, only angle changes.

questions.question: Angles in polar are always 0° to 360°.
questions.explanation: Usually yes, 0° to 360° covers all directions. Sometimes extended beyond.
